name: hfs
description: HFS is a web file server for your computer. Share folders or even a single file thanks to the virtual file system.
readme_summary: HFS (HTTP File Server) is a versatile web-based file server that allows users to share files and folders directly from their computer over the web. It offers features like a virtual file system for flexible sharing, real-time monitoring, bandwidth throttling, and plugin support for extensibility.
primary_use_case: To easily share files and folders from a personal computer over a local network or the internet via a web browser, acting as a personal cloud storage and sharing solution.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot standing in a cozy home office, holding out a glowing digital key. The key is unlocking a portal that reveals a stream of organized files and folders flowing from the robot's computer screen towards a diverse group of people on the other side, who are happily accessing them on their phones and laptops. Subtle data streams and small icons representing different file types are visible. The overall mood is helpful and accessible, with a clean, modern illustration style.

name: lastsignal
description: A self-hosted dead man's switch for delivering encrypted messages (E2EE) to your loved ones — when you're gone or unresponsive.
readme_summary: LastSignal is a self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted dead man's switch that automatically delivers messages to trusted contacts if the user becomes unresponsive. It prioritizes security with a zero-knowledge architecture and modern cryptography, allowing users to configure check-in intervals and delivery delays.
key_features: Configurable timing for check-ins and reminders
primary_use_case: To provide a secure, self-hosted mechanism for users to ensure their important encrypted messages are delivered to loved ones or trusted contacts in the event of their incapacitation or death.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient tree with glowing roots firmly planted in the earth. Periodically, a small, glowing seed pod detaches from the tree and floats gently downwards, representing a scheduled check-in. If the ground around the tree remains undisturbed (no user interaction), a secondary, larger seed pod containing a sealed, encrypted message slowly descends towards a waiting, stylized mailbox labeled 'Trusted Contact'. Subtle digital particles emanate from the tree's trunk, hinting at the underlying technology. The scene is set in a serene, slightly mystical forest clearing with soft, ethereal lighting. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, symbolic aesthetic, emphasizing the concept of a delayed, secure delivery triggered by absence.

name: PDF-Extract-Kit
description: A Comprehensive Toolkit for High-Quality PDF Content Extraction
readme_summary: PDF-Extract-Kit is a Python toolkit for high-quality content extraction from complex PDF documents. It integrates state-of-the-art models for layout detection, formula recognition, OCR, and table recognition, offering a modular design for flexible application building and comprehensive evaluation benchmarks.
key_features: Integration of Leading Document Parsing Models
primary_use_case: Extracting high-quality content and structured data from diverse and complex PDF documents, enabling the development of applications like document translation, Q&A, and assistants.
cover_image_prompt: A master librarian meticulously organizing a chaotic library where books are represented as complex, multi-layered PDF documents. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass that, when focused on a PDF book, reveals its hidden structure: clear text, distinct images, and organized tables, all neatly categorized and extracted onto a nearby workbench. Subtle digital particles and glowing lines emanate from the magnifying glass, symbolizing data extraction. The repository name 'PDF-Extract-Kit' is subtly etched onto the librarian's desk. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting atmosphere with a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and order emerging from complexity.

name: openui
description: OpenUI let's you describe UI using your imagination, then see it rendered live.
readme_summary: OpenUI is an open-source tool that allows users to describe user interfaces using natural language and see them rendered live. It leverages AI to generate UI components and can convert them into various frontend frameworks like React, Svelte, and Web Components.
key_features: Convert HTML to React, Svelte, Web Components, etc.
Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Groq, Gemini, Anthropic, Cohere, Mistral)
Local execution via Docker or from source
primary_use_case: Rapid prototyping and generation of UI components through natural language descriptions, making UI development faster and more flexible.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, imaginative artist sitting at a digital easel, holding a paintbrush that glows with AI energy. With a single stroke, the artist conjures a vibrant, interactive user interface floating in the air before them, complete with buttons, forms, and layouts. Small, stylized code snippets and framework logos (React, Svelte) subtly orbit the UI. The background is a clean, modern studio space with soft, inspiring lighting. The repository name 'OpenUI' is subtly integrated into a digital blueprint on the easel. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a playful, creative aesthetic.
